findings.description: Comprehensive GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline with 18+ workflow files covering all critical quality dimensions including tests, linting, coverage, migrations, documentation, benchmarks, and security checks.
findings.evidence: Workflows found: tests.yml, linters.yml, coverage_tests.yml, coverage_comment.yml, check-migrations.yml, check_commit_messages.yml, check_pr_quality.yml, selenium.yml, postgis.yml, python_matrix.yml, schedule_tests.yml, schedules.yml, docs.yml, benchmark.yml, screenshots.yml. Latest commit from 2026-04-22 shows active CI usage with commits by @jacobtylerwalls, @charettes, @sarahboyce.
findings.recommendation: Maintain current CI/CD excellence. Consider documenting the comprehensive CI strategy for other open-source projects to learn from.

findings.description: Zero technical debt markers found in codebase. Comprehensive linting and formatting tooling configured and enforced.
findings.evidence: Code search query 'repo:django/django TODO FIXME XXX HACK' returned 0 results. Configuration files present: .flake8, .pre-commit-config.yaml, biome.json (JavaScript linting), pyproject.toml with [tool.black] and [tool.isort] configured for Python 3.12+.
findings.recommendation: Continue current code quality practices. The zero-debt approach is exemplary for a codebase of this scale.

findings.description: Exemplary repository organization with clear separation of source code, tests, documentation, and tooling. Modern Python packaging standards implemented.
findings.evidence: Root structure includes: django/ (source), tests/ (comprehensive test suite), docs/ (documentation), scripts/, extras/, js_tests/. Standard files present: README.rst, LICENSE, AUTHORS (45KB), CONTRIBUTING.rst, MANIFEST.in, pyproject.toml. Editor config (.editorconfig), git hygiene (.git-blame-ignore-revs), and comprehensive tooling (tox.ini, Gruntfile.js, package.json).
findings.recommendation: No changes needed. Repository structure serves as an excellent reference for Python project organization.

findings.description: Modern PEP 621 compliant dependency management with minimal core dependencies and proper separation of optional features.
findings.evidence: pyproject.toml shows: requires-python '>= 3.12', only 3 core dependencies (asgiref>=3.9.1, sqlparse>=0.5.0, tzdata), optional dependencies properly separated (argon2-cffi, bcrypt), setuptools>=77.0.3 pinned for build reproducibility. JavaScript dependencies managed via package.json.
findings.recommendation: Excellent dependency hygiene. Continue minimizing core dependencies to reduce supply chain risk.

findings.description: Significant accumulation of stale open pull requests, some dating back 3-5 years with no recent activity, indicating potential review bottleneck or unclear contribution workflow.
findings.evidence: Open PRs sampled include: #11347 (opened May 2019, last updated March 2021), #15439 (opened Feb 2022, last updated Feb 2022), #15464 (Feb 2022), #15478 (March 2022), #15963 (Aug 2022, marked DRAFT), #16189 (Oct 2022), #15418 (Feb 2022, last update Oct 2022), #15990 (Aug 2022, last update Dec 2022), #16638 (March 2023), #16654 (March 2023). Total 10 stale PRs found in sample.
findings.recommendation: Implement periodic PR triage process to close stale PRs or provide feedback. Add clearer guidelines in CONTRIBUTING.rst about GitHub PR workflow vs Trac ticket workflow to set contributor expectations. Consider monthly automated stale PR notifications.

findings.description: Repository shows active, continuous development with recent commits from multiple core maintainers addressing bugs, features, and improvements.
findings.evidence: Recent commits from 2026-04-22: 'Fixed #37057 -- Adjusted UniqueConstraint handling' by @charettes, 'Fixed #35870 -- Made blank choice label more accessible' by @annalauraw, 'Fixed #36991 -- Raised BadRequest for invalid encodings' by @DineshThumma9, 'Fixed #35943 -- Replaced unload event with pagehide' by @aadeina, 'Fixed #15759 -- Excluded fields by per-object permissions' by @jacobtylerwalls.
findings.recommendation: Continue current maintenance practices. The diversity of contributors and steady commit frequency demonstrates healthy project governance.

findings.description: Comprehensive test infrastructure with dedicated test directory, multiple test runners (unit, Selenium, PostGIS), and coverage tracking workflows.
findings.evidence: Repository structure shows tests/ directory at root level. Workflows include: tests.yml (main suite), selenium.yml (browser tests), postgis.yml (database-specific tests), coverage_tests.yml + coverage_comment.yml (coverage tracking and PR comments), js_tests/ directory for JavaScript testing, tox.ini (2195 bytes) for environment management.
findings.recommendation: Maintain comprehensive test coverage. Consider publishing test coverage metrics as a badge in README.rst for transparency.

findings.description: Well-documented project with dedicated docs directory, ReadTheDocs integration, and clear contribution guidelines, though GitHub PR workflow could be clearer.
findings.evidence: Repository contains: docs/ directory, CONTRIBUTING.rst, README.rst, .readthedocs.yml configuration, AUTHORS file (45KB showing extensive contributor history), pull_request_template.md in .github/, docs.yml workflow for automated documentation building.
findings.recommendation: Add section to CONTRIBUTING.rst explicitly clarifying when to use GitHub PRs vs Trac tickets, and expected response times for community contributions to reduce stale PR accumulation.

findings.description: Django does not use GitHub Issues for bug tracking. The repository has 0 open issues, which initially appears concerning but is intentional.
findings.evidence: API call list_issues returned totalCount=0. CONTRIBUTING.rst states: 'Django uses Trac to keep track of bugs, feature requests, and associated patches because GitHub doesn't provide adequate tooling for its community. Patches can be submitted as pull requests, but if you don't file a ticket, it's unlikely that we'll notice your contribution.'
findings.recommendation: This is a deliberate architectural choice. New contributors should file tickets at https://code.djangoproject.com/newticket before submitting non-trivial PRs. Consider adding a GitHub Issue template that redirects to Trac for better discoverability.

findings.description: Excellent contributor diversity with 8 unique authors in the 10 most recent commits, indicating healthy distributed development.
findings.evidence: list_commits API call shows authors: Simon Charette (@charettes), Annabelle Wiegart (@annalauraw), Dinesh (@DineshThumma9), Amar (@aadeina), Artyom Kotovskiy, Clifford Gama (@cliffordgama), Mariusz Felisiak (@felixxm), Jacob Walls (@jacobtylerwalls), varunkasyap (@varunkasyap). Commits dated April 2026 with messages like 'Fixed #37057', 'Fixed #35870', 'Fixed #36991'.
findings.recommendation: Continue current practices. The mix of established and new contributors demonstrates effective onboarding and community growth.

findings.description: Low bus factor risk with multiple active committers. Jacob Walls appears as committer on 7/10 recent commits, but different authors contribute code, indicating mentorship and review process.
findings.evidence: Committers in recent history include Jacob Walls (@jacobtylerwalls), Sarah Boyce (@sarahboyce), and GitHub web-flow. Multiple co-authored commits visible: 'Co-Authored-By: Marijke Luttekes' on commit 63c56cd, 'Co-authored-by: Peter van der Does' on commit e1bdebc.
findings.recommendation: Healthy distribution of maintainer responsibilities. Continue encouraging co-authorship and review practices.

findings.description: Exemplary governance with comprehensive documentation. CONTRIBUTING.rst file (1,115 bytes) provides clear guidance and links to extensive online documentation.
findings.evidence: Root directory contains CONTRIBUTING.rst stating: 'Extensive contribution guidelines are available in the repository at docs/internals/contributing/, or online at: https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/dev/internals/contributing/'. Code of Conduct referenced: 'Please read and follow our Code of Conduct <https://www.djangoproject.com/conduct/>'. Code search found docs/internals/organization.txt confirming formal governance structure. AUTHORS file is 45,163 bytes.
findings.recommendation: Best-in-class governance model. No changes needed.

findings.description: Outstanding release discipline with 600+ documented releases spanning nearly 20 years. Active maintenance across multiple version branches.
findings.evidence: docs/releases/ directory contains release notes from version 0.95 through 6.1. Recent releases: 6.0.5.txt (latest), 6.0.4.txt, 6.0.3.txt, 6.0.2.txt, 6.0.1.txt, 6.0.txt (25,030 bytes). LTS version 4.2 has 30 patch releases (4.2.1.txt through 4.2.30.txt). Version 5.2 series shows 13 releases. Security documentation file at docs/releases/security.txt is 70,747 bytes.
findings.recommendation: Continue exemplary release practices. The predictable cadence and long-term support strategy demonstrate exceptional project maturity.

findings.description: Comprehensive documentation structure with dedicated docs/ directory containing internals, releases, and extensive guides.
findings.evidence: Root directory listing shows docs/ directory containing: internals/, releases/, howto/, ref/, topics/, faq/. README.rst file present (2,017 bytes). .readthedocs.yml configuration file confirms integrated documentation hosting.
findings.recommendation: Documentation is excellent. Consider adding a brief note in the main README about the Trac workflow to help GitHub-native users.

findings.description: Professional development infrastructure with CI/CD, pre-commit hooks, and quality tooling in place.
findings.evidence: Root directory contains: .pre-commit-config.yaml (797 bytes), .github/ directory, .editorconfig (697 bytes), .flake8 (297 bytes), tox.ini (2,195 bytes), biome.json (1,699 bytes), pyproject.toml (2,167 bytes).
findings.recommendation: Infrastructure is production-grade. Continue maintaining high code quality standards.

findings.description: High-quality commit messages with issue references and descriptive content, demonstrating professional development practices.
findings.evidence: Recent commit messages show clear issue tracking: 'Fixed #37057 -- Adjusted UniqueConstraint handling of UNKNOWN condition' (61a62be), 'Fixed #35870 -- Made blank choice label in forms more accessible' (63c56cd), 'Fixed #36991 -- Raised BadRequest for invalid encodings in Content-Type headers' (dc467fd), 'Fixed #15759 -- Excluded fields by per-object permissions for ModelAdmin.list_editable' (84db026).
findings.recommendation: Commit message quality is excellent. Continue enforcing issue references in commit messages.

findings.description: Excellent README.rst file present with comprehensive coverage of all essential topics. The README is 2,017 bytes and provides clear project description, installation guidance, tutorial references, deployment instructions, community links, contribution guidelines, and testing instructions.
findings.evidence: File: README.rst (2,017 bytes, SHA: b7532e6b5ad352fe5d560962f8c59356d2424fae). Content includes: project description ('Django is a high-level Python web framework'), documentation links (https://docs.djangoproject.com/), tutorial series references (docs/intro/tutorial01.txt through tutorial08.txt), deployment guide pointer (docs/howto/deployment/index.txt), community links (Discord, Forum), and contribution guide (https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/dev/internals/contributing/).
findings.recommendation: No action required. README is comprehensive and professional. Optional: Consider adding status badges (build, coverage) for quick project health visibility.

findings.description: World-class API and code documentation infrastructure with dedicated docs/ directory containing comprehensive Sphinx-based documentation. The documentation is professionally organized with multiple content types (tutorials, topics, how-tos, API reference) and integrated with ReadTheDocs for online hosting.
findings.evidence: Repository tree shows docs/ directory (SHA: a611ecf8a3d07950fe7edebd9b4626dcaf8a6ddc) containing: conf.py (14,810 bytes - Sphinx config), Makefile (7,125 bytes), subdirectories including ref/ (API reference), topics/ (topical guides), howto/ (how-to guides), intro/ (tutorials), internals/ (contributing docs), faq/, and releases/. ReadTheDocs configuration file present (.readthedocs.yml, 410 bytes). Online documentation available at https://docs.djangoproject.com/.
findings.recommendation: No action required. Documentation infrastructure is exemplary and represents industry best practice for large open-source projects.

findings.description: Exceptional changelog and release notes management with 200+ individual release note files spanning 18+ years of development. Every version from 0.95 to 6.1+ is documented with dedicated security tracking.
findings.evidence: Directory docs/releases/ contains 200+ release files including major versions (1.0.txt through 6.1.txt), all patch releases (e.g., 4.2.1.txt through 4.2.30.txt, 5.1.1.txt through 5.1.15.txt), index.txt (6,172 bytes - organized TOC), and security.txt (70,747 bytes - comprehensive security changelog). Files range from version 0.95 (5,716 bytes) to current 6.1.txt (18,770 bytes). Consistent naming convention: [version].txt format.
findings.recommendation: No action required. Changelog management is exemplary with comprehensive historical coverage and clear organization.

findings.description: Comprehensive contribution documentation with multiple levels of detail. Primary CONTRIBUTING.rst file (1,115 bytes) provides clear overview and process requirements, supported by extensive 23KB+ detailed guide in docs/intro/contributing.txt. Clear ticket-based workflow using Trac issue tracker.
findings.evidence: File CONTRIBUTING.rst (1,115 bytes, SHA: 4b2ab363660df5b4b43e8e263b70ddb96b1db54e) includes contribution types (code patches, documentation, bug reports), clear warning about PR requirements ('non-trivial pull requests without Trac tickets will be closed'), links to extended guide (docs/internals/contributing/), and Code of Conduct reference. Extended guide: docs/intro/contributing.txt (23,718 bytes) provides comprehensive contribution instructions.
findings.recommendation: No action required. Contribution process is clearly documented with appropriate detail levels. The Trac requirement is explicitly stated to prevent wasted effort.

findings.description: Outstanding tutorial series with 8 comprehensive, progressive tutorials totaling over 140KB of educational content. Tutorials cover the complete development lifecycle from installation through advanced topics like testing and deployment.
findings.evidence: Directory docs/intro/ contains 8 tutorial files: tutorial01.txt (10,844 bytes - getting started), tutorial02.txt (26,180 bytes - database/admin), tutorial03.txt (18,075 bytes - views/templates), tutorial04.txt (14,218 bytes - forms), tutorial05.txt (28,460 bytes - testing), tutorial06.txt (5,508 bytes - static files), tutorial07.txt (16,289 bytes - customizing admin), tutorial08.txt (4,707 bytes - third-party packages). Additional resources include overview.txt (12,922 bytes), install.txt (2,808 bytes), reusable-apps.txt (14,368 bytes), whatsnext.txt (9,095 bytes), and _images/ directory for tutorial visuals.
findings.recommendation: No action required. Tutorial series is comprehensive, well-structured, and covers both beginner and advanced topics progressively.

mcp_params.query: repo:django/django path:.github/workflows codeql OR bandit OR semgrep OR security

findings.description: Security policy is properly documented and references Django's established responsible disclosure process
findings.evidence: File .github/SECURITY.md exists with content: 'Please see https://www.djangoproject.com/security/.' This links to Django's comprehensive security policy maintained by the Django Security Team.
findings.recommendation: Consider enhancing SECURITY.md with inline contact information (security email) and expected response times rather than only external links for easier discoverability.

findings.description: No dedicated security-focused static analysis tools (CodeQL, Bandit, Semgrep) detected in CI workflows
findings.evidence: Search query 'repo:django/django path:.github/workflows codeql OR bandit OR semgrep OR security' returned 0 results. Workflow file .github/workflows/linters.yml contains only code quality tools: flake8, isort, black, biome. No Python security scanners found.
findings.recommendation: Integrate Bandit for Python security linting to detect hardcoded secrets, SQL injection risks, and unsafe function usage. Consider adding CodeQL for semantic security analysis or Semgrep with OWASP rulesets for web framework-specific vulnerabilities.

findings.description: No publicly visible dependency vulnerability scanning configuration (Dependabot or Renovate)
findings.evidence: File check for .github/dependabot.yml returned error: 'The path does not point to a file or directory, or the file does not exist in the repository.' No Dependabot configuration found in repository.
findings.recommendation: Enable GitHub Dependabot with security-only updates at minimum. Configure for pip ecosystem to receive automated alerts and pull requests for vulnerable dependencies. This is critical for a web framework with widespread deployment.

findings.description: Minimal usage of potentially dangerous subprocess patterns found in codebase
findings.evidence: Search query 'repo:django/django shell=True language:python' returned 2 results: django/utils/version.py and scripts/manage_translations.py. Only 2 occurrences of shell=True parameter found, limited to utility scripts rather than core framework code.
findings.recommendation: Review the 2 instances of shell=True usage in django/utils/version.py and scripts/manage_translations.py to ensure all inputs are properly sanitized. Consider refactoring to use shell=False with argument lists where possible.

findings.description: GitHub Actions workflow security includes zizmor scanning but mixed SHA pinning practices
findings.evidence: Workflow .github/workflows/linters.yml includes zizmor action (zizmorcore/zizmor-action@e639db99335bc9038abc0e066dfcd72e23d26fb4) for GitHub Actions security scanning. However, mixed pinning observed: actions/checkout@v6 and actions/setup-python@v6 use mutable tags, while zizmor-action and liskin/gh-problem-matcher-wrap@e7b7beaaafa52524748b31a381160759d68d61fb use SHA pinning.
findings.recommendation: Pin all third-party GitHub Actions to commit SHAs instead of mutable tags to prevent tag mutation attacks. Use Dependabot for GitHub Actions to automatically update pinned SHAs while maintaining supply-chain integrity.

findings.description: Repository includes zizmor for GitHub Actions workflow security auditing
findings.evidence: File .github/workflows/linters.yml contains job 'zizmor' using zizmorcore/zizmor-action@e639db99335bc9038abc0e066dfcd72e23d26fb4 with annotations enabled for workflow security analysis.
findings.recommendation: Maintain zizmor integration and review its findings regularly. This provides good protection against common GitHub Actions security misconfigurations.

findings.description: Comprehensive code quality and linting tools integrated in CI pipeline
findings.evidence: Workflow .github/workflows/linters.yml shows integration of flake8, isort, black, and biome for code quality. Multiple workflows found including docs.yml, labels.yml, schedules.yml, python_matrix.yml, benchmark.yml, linters.yml indicating mature CI/CD practices.
findings.recommendation: Code quality tooling is strong. Consider complementing these with security-focused linters to achieve comprehensive coverage.

findings.description: Workflow follows least-privilege principle with read-only content permissions
findings.evidence: File .github/workflows/linters.yml contains 'permissions: contents: read' declaration, limiting workflow permissions to read-only access.
findings.recommendation: Excellent practice. Ensure all workflows follow this pattern and only escalate permissions when absolutely necessary with explicit justification.

findings.description: Primary LICENSE file present at repository root with BSD 3-Clause License. The license clearly states copyright holder as 'Django Software Foundation and individual contributors' and includes complete BSD 3-Clause terms.
findings.evidence: File path: /LICENSE, SHA: 5f4f225dd282aa7e4361ec3c2750bbbaaed8ab1f, Size: 1,552 bytes. Content begins: 'Copyright (c) Django Software Foundation and individual contributors. All rights reserved. Redistribution and use in source and binary forms, with or without modification, are permitted provided that the following conditions are met...'
findings.recommendation: Maintain the current LICENSE file and keep it updated with any changes to copyright holders or license terms.

findings.description: Additional LICENSE.python file present for proper attribution of Python-derived code, along with comprehensive AUTHORS file. This demonstrates excellent third-party attribution practices.
findings.evidence: Repository tree shows: LICENSE.python (14,256 bytes, SHA: 2fc28e876fa1fc8c536f46b81789e2bb51b323c0), AUTHORS (45,163 bytes, SHA: 07c87068a9d3b46491391818053839dcd5ffcf1e). Both files present in root directory.
findings.recommendation: Continue maintaining the AUTHORS file with contributor updates and preserve LICENSE.python for Python standard library code attribution.

findings.description: License properly declared in pyproject.toml using SPDX identifier BSD-3-Clause with explicit license-files array listing all license and attribution files.
findings.evidence: pyproject.toml content (SHA: c64cd28dab46fc6f1a24ef3e963cd6bd54186c93): 'license = "BSD-3-Clause"' and 'license-files = ["LICENSE", "LICENSE.python", "AUTHORS"]'. Authors declared as: 'Django Software Foundation, foundation@djangoproject.com'
findings.recommendation: Maintain current metadata structure. Ensure any future license files are added to the license-files array.

findings.description: Source files do not contain license headers or copyright notices. While BSD-3-Clause license does not strictly require per-file headers, this represents a minor deviation from best practices for large open-source projects.
findings.evidence: Examined django/__init__.py (SHA: 4402600c576fab7d8e88389799558aa963369b2d) - no license header present. Code search 'repo:django/django "Copyright (c) Django Software Foundation" language:Python' returned 0 results. Code search 'repo:django/django "All rights reserved" OR "BSD" path:*.py' returned 0 results (total_count: 0, incomplete_results: false).
findings.recommendation: Consider adding minimal SPDX headers to source files for improved clarity: '# SPDX-License-Identifier: BSD-3-Clause'. This is optional for BSD licenses but enhances license traceability.

findings.description: All declared dependencies use permissive licenses that are fully compatible with BSD-3-Clause distribution. No copyleft or restrictive licenses detected in the dependency chain.
findings.evidence: Dependencies from pyproject.toml: 'asgiref>=3.9.1' (BSD License), 'sqlparse>=0.5.0' (BSD License), 'tzdata; sys_platform == "win32"' (Apache 2.0), optional: 'argon2-cffi>=23.1.0' (MIT), 'bcrypt>=4.1.1' (Apache 2.0). All are permissive licenses compatible with BSD-3-Clause.
findings.recommendation: Continue vetting new dependencies for license compatibility. Document any dependencies with non-standard licenses.

findings.description: Project is backed by the Django Software Foundation, a legal entity that holds copyright and manages intellectual property. This provides strong legal foundation and governance structure.
findings.evidence: LICENSE file copyright: 'Copyright (c) Django Software Foundation and individual contributors'. pyproject.toml authors: 'Django Software Foundation, email: foundation@djangoproject.com'. CONTRIBUTING.rst file present (1,115 bytes, SHA: 4b2ab363660df5b4b43e8e263b70ddb96b1db54e).
findings.recommendation: Maintain clear communication about contributor license agreements (CLAs) and ensure the Foundation's governance documents remain accessible.
